pr.title: Phase 4c: remove flat-file compatibility shims and switch call sites to core modules
pr.body:
## Summary

Completes Phase 4c by removing root flat-file compatibility shims and moving all remaining imports to canonical `core.*` modules. This resolves lint failures caused by CI still targeting deleted shim paths.

## Linked issue

Issue linking is handled automatically by the system.

## What changed

- **Import graph cleanup (repo-wide)**
  - Repointed call sites from shim modules (`models`, `media_inspect`, `audio_utils`, `subtitle_utils`, `srt_writer`, `asr`, `mt`, `llm_polish`, `compare_core`, `tracing`, `benchmark`) to `core.*` equivalents across runtime code, packs, utilities, and tests.
  - Example import migration:
    ```python
    # before
    from models import SubtitleCandidate
    from mt import translate_candidate_jp_to_en_workflow

    # after
    from core.subtitles import SubtitleCandidate
    from core.mt import translate_candidate_jp_to_en_workflow
    ```

- **Shim deletion**
  - Removed root shim files:
    - `models.py`, `media_inspect.py`, `tracing.py`, `compare_core.py`
    - `audio_utils.py`, `subtitle_utils.py`, `srt_writer.py`
    - `asr.py`, `mt.py`, `llm_polish.py`, `benchmark.py`

- **CI/lint target updates**
  - Updated lint file list in `.github/workflows/ci.yml` to target current `core/*` module files rather than deleted shim files.
  - Synced local contributor command docs (`CLAUDE.md`, `CONTRIBUTING.md`) with the new lint targets.

- **Migration-note cleanup**
  - Removed stale “root shim during migration” references in affected core module docstrings where paths are now canonicalized.

## Tests

- [ ] New tests added for new behavior
- [x] Existing tests updated where behavior changed
- [x] All tests pass locally (`pytest`)
- [ ] If no tests apply, reason documented here: N/A — tests apply and were run.

## Acceptance checklist

- [x] Acceptance criteria for the linked issue are met (reference `acceptance/` doc or paste criteria inline below)
- [ ] `docs/` updated if architecture, module boundaries, or design decisions changed
- [ ] `specs/` updated or created if this implements a spec
- [ ] `prompts/` updated if this introduces or changes a reusable AI prompt
- [ ] `acceptance/` updated if this changes or defines acceptance criteria for a feature

**Acceptance criteria status:**

- Root flat-file shims targeted in Phase 4c are removed.
- Imports are redirected to `core.*` module paths.
- CI lint references removed shim paths no longer and points at current core modules.

Notes on unchecked items:
- `docs/`: no architecture/design change; this is execution of existing migration plan (only command references were updated in contributor docs).
- `specs/`, `prompts/`, `acceptance/`: no new feature contract or acceptance definition introduced.

## AI usage disclosure

- [ ] AI tooling was NOT used for this change
- [x] AI tooling WAS used — details below:

**Tool(s) used:**
- GitHub Copilot coding agent

**What was generated:**
- Bulk import-path refactors, shim file removals, CI lint target updates, and minor docstring cleanup.

**How it was validated (tests run, diff reviewed, etc.):**
- Reviewed resulting diff for module-path correctness and executed repository test suite/lint checks locally in the task environment.

**Assumptions made by the AI that affect design or scope:**
- Phase 4c scope is limited to shim removal + import redirection + CI lint path alignment; no behavioral changes intended.

## Risks and follow-up

- Remaining docs outside contributor workflow docs may still reference removed root modules; optional follow-up sweep can normalize those references.
pr.author: Copilot
